title: CHANNEL variable is empty in the same step where it is written to GITHUB_ENV
reasoning: GitHub Actions only exposes variables written to $GITHUB_ENV in *subsequent* steps, not the current one. On line 132, `${CHANNEL}` expands to an empty string because the `echo "CHANNEL=..." >> "$GITHUB_ENV"` on lines 126/128/130 does not update the current shell environment. The log line will always read `CHANNEL=` with no value, making the diagnostic output misleading.
reproduction: Push any tag matching v*.*.* and observe the 'Set CHANNEL' step log; CHANNEL will print as empty.
recommendation: Set a local shell variable in addition to writing to GITHUB_ENV, e.g. `CHANNEL=stable; echo "CHANNEL=$CHANNEL" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"` and then reference the local variable in the echo on line 132.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: There are no tests for this workflow file, and the bug is cosmetic (log output only), so it would not be caught by any functional test.
minimumFixScope: Introduce a local shell variable (e.g. `CHANNEL=...`) before the GITHUB_ENV write and use it in the echo on line 132.
